div.h2: Ch(x, y, z) = (x AND y) XOR (NOT x AND z)

p.strong: Bitwise Multiplexer — Single Bit Position Analysis

div.p: FIPS 180-4 §4.1.2, Equation (4.2) Ch(x, y, z) = (x AND y) XOR (NOT x AND z) Ternary reading: x ? y : z (applied bit-by-bit) Nonlinear cross-term: output contains xy (AND of two inputs) → cannot be expressed as ax ⊕ by ⊕ cz for any constants a, b, c

g.text: x │ y │ z │ x AND y │ NOT x AND z │ Ch │ Interpretation

div.p: Why Ch is Nonlinear over GF(2) A linear function satisfies: f(a ⊕ b) = f(a) ⊕ f(b) Ch contains AND(x, y) — multiplication in GF(2). Multiplications produce cross-terms (e.g., xy) that cannot be expressed as any linear combination ax ⊕ by ⊕ cz. Proof sketch: Ch(1,0,0) = 0, Ch(0,1,0) = 0 Ch(1⊕0, 0⊕1, 0) = Ch(1,1,0) = 1 But Ch(1,0,0) ⊕ Ch(0,1,0) = 0 ⊕ 0 = 0 ≠ 1 → XOR-linearity fails → Ch is nonlinear ✓
